title: SIWO Forecast – April 18, 2025
summary: Assessment of Current Ice Conditions Relevant to Distribution and Access of Walrus Click the name of each community below to view more frequently updated and detailed information from the National Weather Service. Synopsis : A low-pressure system over western Alaska and the Bering Sea will shift to the north through Saturday evening, bringing windy, blizzard conditions along the North Slope and will begin to dwindle off Friday evening. A low pressure will be following a slight ridge and will begin to impact the Y-K Delta Sunday morning by bringing mostly rain in, though some places will have snow or a mix of both. As this occurs, a high pressure will build on the North Slope, keeping the high temperatures in the low-to-mid double digits and the low temperatures in the negative single digits all week. title: Aleut Real Estate Invests in The Wildbirch Hotel  to Revitalize Downtown Anchorage
Arctic Sea Ice Extent: April 17, 2025
summary: April 17, 2025: The Arctic sea ice has experienced an extremely small decrease for this week of 0.04% (last week’s decrease rate was 0.14%), from the level on 04/10/2025 of 13.959 M km 2 to 13.954 M km 2 for this week. Plot Compiled by: Howard J. Diamond, PhD; Climate Science Program Manager at NOAA...

title: Wrangell schools face fewer teachers and class changes next year
summary: Voc Ed teacher Winston Davies speaks to Wrangell School Board at Evergreen Elementary on April 14, 2025. He discusses with them that he was unaware of the school district changing voc ed classes from required to electives. Odile Meister just learned that she will lose her job at both Evergreen Elementary and Wrangell High School. The classes are different, though. Due to a looming budget deficit, Wrangell Public School District will slash at least two positions next year. Additionally, some changes with required classes include transitioning them to electives...

title: AST VCOR, Unlawful Contact: North Pole [AK25036504]
summary: On April 18, 2025, at approximately 1344 hours, the Alaska State Troopers responded to the area of Groundsel Ave in North Pole for a report of individuals parked on private property. Investigation revealed that Andrew Sexton was in violation of conditions of release and in contact with the victim in...
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST Take Lynx Closed Season and Unlawful Possession: Seward [AK25030905]
incident_type: Take Lynx Closed Season and Unlawful Possession
summary: On 4/3/2025 at approximately 1730 hours, Alaska Wildlife Troopers and Alaska State Troopers responded to a report of a lynx being taken in closed season at the Ptarmigan Creek Trailhead near Seward. Investigation revealed the lynx was shot by Gage Ryan Koehn, age 19 of Soldotna. Koehn said he was un...
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch
topic: Take Lynx Closed Season and Unlawful Possession

title: AST DUI/Reckless Endangerment: Fairbanks [AK25035785]
summary: On 04/16/2025, at approximately 2248 hours, Steven Reeves called to report that the mother of his child, Victoria Legerat, left their house in a black Escalade and was under the influence of alcohol. Victoria was contacted at the 1230 Hayes Avenue operating the vehicle with the child in the backseat. Victoria was arrested for DUI and reckless endangerment.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

entities.address_detail: the 1230 Hayes Avenue operating the vehicle with the child in

title: AST Violation of Protection Order: Fairbanks [AK25036275]
summary: On April 18, 2025, at approximately 0322 hours, Fairbanks AST received a report of an assault. The investigation revealed probable cause to arrest Christopher Coke for violating a protective order. Christopher was remanded to Fairbanks Correctional Center without incident.

entities.address_detail: revealed probable cause to arrest Christopher Coke for violating a protective order
